mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand expansion, driving corporate revenue expectations higher and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative mean 20-day returns in > 60% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses predictive power (t-stat < 1.5) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect expanding manufacturing capacity utilization, which translates into higher corporate profit margins and positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns during recovery phases, or the signal reverses direction when inflation exceeds 3.5%.

mechanism_narrative: The co-occurrence of elevated federal funds rates above 4.0% and unemployment above 4.0% indicates restrictive monetary policy straining a weakening labor market, forcing equity deleveraging and negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined threshold condition yields positive 20-day returns in > 50% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.0% annualized confirms macroeconomic expansion, reinforcing earnings forecasts and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days following data release.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no correlation with 5-day forward returns, or positive GDP surprises trigger negative market reactions in > 40% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase exceeding 0.4% signals accelerating inflationary pressures, prompting expectations of further monetary tightening that compresses equity valuations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes above 0.4% are followed by positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances, or the signal loses predictive accuracy when real yields are negative.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent monthly increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate demonstrate robust labor market absorption, supporting consumer spending and generating positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint labor signal produces negative 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered months, or the combined effect fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline in out-of-sample ranking.
